days.title: Arrival / Alignment / Kickoff
days.theme: Welcome home, one team, set the tone
days.attire: Travel casual → Austin Chic for party

sessions.title: Staff Travel to Austin
sessions.description: Out-of-town and STATION staff travel to Austin. Local staff report to STATION Austin Downtown.
sessions.logistics: Omni Hotel block for lodging

sessions.title: Light Bites — Mingle & Headshots
sessions.description: Staff arrive, grab food, catch up, get professional headshots taken. Babson Diagnostics arrives ~noon to set up blood-testing activation in a separate area.
sessions.logistics: Catering setup by 11:30 AM. Photographer station needed.

sessions.title: All Staff Photo
sessions.description: Annual group photo of the entire Dreamteam. Used for internal comms, social media, and the DCS26 highlight video.
sessions.logistics: Need photographer + designated spot (lobby or Voltron stage)

sessions.title: Mingle continues
sessions.description: Buffer before programming starts. Staff finish headshots and settle in.

sessions.title: Welcome & Run of Show Overview
sessions.description: Kyle kicks off DREAMCON — quick overview of the week: what's happening each day, ground rules, logistics, and expectations. Sets the energy.
sessions.logistics: Need slide with 4-day overview

sessions.title: FOUNDER STORY: Babson Diagnostics
sessions.description: Portfolio company founder shares their journey building Babson Diagnostics. 15 min presentation + 5 min Q&A. Reminds the team why Capital Factory exists — we back founders building real things. Blood-testing demo available during breaks.
sessions.logistics: Presenter needs AV/screen. Confirm deck format.

sessions.title: Break
sessions.description: Babson blood-testing demo available in a separate area for interested staff.

sessions.title: Safety Reminders
sessions.description: Quick building safety/security overview — emergency exits, rally points, emergency contacts, after-hours access. Especially important for out-of-town STATION staff unfamiliar with STATION Austin Downtown.

sessions.title: CORE VALUES
sessions.description: Josh introduces CF's 6 core values (5 min) then each of last DREAMCON's 6 Core Value Award winners speaks ~3 min on what their value means to them personally. Audience participates — share examples of how you've seen others embody each value. This is culture-setting, not a lecture.
sessions.logistics: Winners from DCW25: Aubrey, Jonathan Randall, Naomi, Sofia Wynn, Valerie, Eli Springer

sessions.title: New Faces
sessions.description: Each team member who joined since DCW25 introduces themselves — name, role, team, one fun fact. 30 seconds each, hard cap. Kyle keeps time. A moment for new people to be seen and welcomed by the full Dreamteam.
sessions.logistics: Get list of new hires from HR. Prep name order.

sessions.title: STARTUP CUP — Intro, Team Assignments + Game 1
sessions.description: Multi-day team competition kicks off. Kyle explains the format, ~10 teams are assigned (balanced across CF/STATION, departments, tenure, DISC types), teams pick names. Game 1 starts immediately. Running leaderboard tracked all week.
sessions.logistics: Teams pre-built. Scoring spreadsheet ready. Prizes purchased.

sessions.title: Install Claude Code — Live Tutorial
sessions.description: Every staff member installs Claude Code on their machine — terminal for Mac, PowerShell for Windows. Hands-on, everyone follows along step by step. By the end, every Dreamteamer has a working AI coding agent on their laptop.
sessions.logistics: Confirm wifi capacity. Have troubleshooting helpers ready.

sessions.title: delegation.school (AI Workflow Training)
sessions.description: Josh walks everyone through his AI workflow training course (delegation.school). Staff already have Claude Code installed from the previous session. This session pulls out certain examples people have done and shows others what is possible. Also used to gather feedback.
sessions.logistics: Confirm wifi can handle all staff on Claude Code simultaneously.

sessions.title: Alumni Spotlight #1
sessions.description: Former CF/STATION team member returns to share their post-CF career story — where they went, what they built, how CF prepared them. A reminder that Capital Factory is a launchpad for careers.
sessions.logistics: Reach out 6+ weeks in advance.

sessions.title: Closing Remarks
sessions.description: Mary wraps Day 1 — quick recap, preview of tomorrow, logistics for the party.

sessions.title: DREAMTEAM PARTY + Awards & Anniversaries
sessions.description: Staff social at Daydreamer (E 6th St). Rumi Lane Embroidery station: staff bring 1 clothing item to get personally stitched OR get a comfort color tee with STATION or CF on back/sleeve + name on pocket. Photobooth, spin-the-wheel prizes, DJ. Awards & Anniversaries: tenure milestones recognized with tiered gifts (2yr coin, 3yr keyring, 4yr book, 5yr Tiffany necklace/cufflinks). This is the culture highlight of DREAMCON.
sessions.logistics: Venue: Daydreamer (E 6th St). DJ, photobooth, Rumi Lane booked. Anniversary gifts purchased and ready.

days.title: North Stars / Leadership Reports / AI Con

sessions.title: FOUNDER STORY: Bravo Ordnance
sessions.description: Portfolio company founder shares their journey building in the defense/munitions space. 15 min presentation + 5 min Q&A.
sessions.logistics: Confirm presenter + deck

sessions.title: Walk & Talk Round 1 — Someone on your team you work closely with
sessions.description: Paired walking conversation. Each pair gets a prompt: 'Describe what you like most about your job and the moments when you feel the best.' Get outside if weather permits. Builds deeper bonds within existing teams.
sessions.logistics: Pairings finalized 1 week out. Print handouts or post on Slack.

sessions.label: Josh does CF first, walks to STATION after break

sessions.title: CF NORTH STAR
sessions.description: Josh's strategic keynote for Capital Factory — where the venture/investment side is headed, key priorities for H2 2026, what's changing, what's staying the course. This is the single most important session for CF staff.
sessions.logistics: Josh deck finalized 1 week out. COS does prep session with Josh.

sessions.title: What We Do & What We're Proud Of
sessions.description: CF department leads share their team's wins and vision. Framework: one simple thing we can do now, one dream thing we're working toward, one immediate thing we need help with.

sessions.title: CF LEADERSHIP REPORTS
sessions.description: Each CF department presents: what the team does, big wins since DCW25, key metrics, what's coming next. 7 departments x 5 min + buffer. Canva deck format. Strict 5-min limit with visible timer.
sessions.logistics: CF Canva template. Timer visible. 7 depts: All Access Fund, Texas Fund, Fellowship Fund, Venture Programs, IR, Customer Engagement, Gov Programs

sessions.title: STATION IDENTITY
sessions.description: Mary facilitates a dedicated session on STATION's brand and identity. What makes STATION different from CF? How do we differentiate? What are we most proud of? Directly addresses STATION team feedback that they need their own identity and morale building. This is STATION's moment.
sessions.logistics: Mary texts/Slacks Josh the top 3 themes by 10:30 so he has them before STATION North Star.

sessions.title: Break — Josh walks over from Captain America
STATION NORTH STAR
sessions.description: Josh's strategic keynote for STATION — acknowledging STATION's unique value, where operations/coworking is headed, what makes STATION special. Morale and pride building. Josh should reference themes from Mary's STATION Identity session.
sessions.logistics: Josh prep: Mary will text/Slack top themes from her 10:00 session. Review during the break.

sessions.title: STATION LEADERSHIP REPORTS
sessions.description: 9 departments present individually (5 min each = 45 min). Smallest 2-3 depts (Gifts & Gear, CDUI, STATION DC) share a single combined slide (5 min total). 50 min total. Strict 5-min limit with visible timer. Canva deck format.
sessions.logistics: STATION Canva templates. Individual: Sales, Commons, Systems, Mentor, Marketing, Events, Venue, Workplace, VIP. Shared slide: Gifts & Gear, CDUI, STATION DC

sessions.title: Lunch & Emails
sessions.description: Protected midday break. Staff catch up on email and recharge.

sessions.title: Walk & Talk Round 2 — Someone on another team you work with
sessions.description: Paired with someone on a different team. Prompt: 'Share something about your life outside of work — what you're into, where you're from, what matters to you.' Depth is optional — go as deep as feels natural. Builds real human connection across departments and entities.
sessions.logistics: Pairings ensure CF/STATION staff are mixed.

sessions.title: Startup Cup Game 2
sessions.description: Quick 10-min team game to keep energy high.

sessions.title: Claude Code Troubleshooting & Advanced Setup
sessions.description: Troubleshooting for anyone who had issues on Day 1. Advanced setup tips, MCP servers, custom commands. Helpers float to unblock stuck machines.
sessions.logistics: Have troubleshooting helpers ready. Track who still needs help before MVP build.

sessions.title: STARTUP CUP — Build an MVP
sessions.description: The AI Con centerpiece. Each Startup Cup team gets 90 min to build a working web app using Claude Code. Fun app ideas provided: CF Compliment Generator / DREAMCON Bingo / Lunch Decider / Who Said It? / STATION Tour Guide. Teams submit their URL to COSEA/Josh for judging. Winner announced Day 3. This is where staff see AI go from concept to a real shipped product — in 90 minutes.
sessions.logistics: Have app idea cards printed. Submission form ready. Seed each team with at least one person who completed Day 1 delegation.school. Float troubleshooters during the build.

sessions.title: Alumni Spotlight #2
sessions.description: Former CF/STATION team member shares their post-CF career journey.

sessions.title: Closing Remarks
sessions.description: Eric wraps Day 2 — recap of the AI builds, preview of Day 3.

days.title: AI Tools / Interlocks / Dept Working Sessions

sessions.title: Welcome & Breakfast
sessions.description: Kyle opens Day 3 and announces the Startup Cup MVP winner from Day 2's app build. Big points awarded.
sessions.logistics: Have winner ready + demo URL on screen

sessions.title: Alumni Spotlight #3
sessions.description: Former CF/STATION team member shares their post-CF career journey.

sessions.title: Walk & Talk Round 3 — Someone you don't typically interact with
sessions.description: Final Walk & Talk round. Paired with someone you don't normally interact with — intentionally crosses CF/STATION lines. Prompt: 'Describe your job at CF/STATION.' Builds understanding of what other teams actually do day-to-day.
sessions.logistics: Pairings ensure maximum cross-entity mixing.

sessions.title: Josh's Tools — Live Tutorials
sessions.description: Live tutorials on the AI tools Josh uses and has built: RelRadar (relationship intelligence), MultiPOV (multi-perspective analysis), Trademarc (trademark search), Witt (writing assistant), and others. Josh demos each tool, explains when to use it, and staff follow along. Practical AI adoption — not theory, real tools for real work.
sessions.logistics: Ensure tools are accessible to all staff. Have login/access ready.

sessions.title: CROSS-ENTITY INTERLOCK
sessions.description: CF and STATION staff work together in small mixed groups on shared challenges. Every group has a facilitator, notetaker, and must produce a defined outcome with owners and deadlines. Topics: collaboration on cornerstone events, shared workflows, automation opportunities. This is the #1 priority from CF feedback — building real working rapport between the two entities.
sessions.logistics: Interlock submissions collected in advance. Room assignments posted. Outcome templates distributed.

sessions.title: Startup Cup Game 3
sessions.description: Quick 10-min team game to break up the day and keep energy high.

sessions.title: Lunch & Emails
sessions.description: Protected midday break.

sessions.title: INTERLOCK Round 2
sessions.description: CF-only interlocks. Smaller groups than past DREAMCONs (per feedback that groups were too big). Every interlock has a facilitator, notetaker, and must produce a defined outcome with owners and deadlines. This is where real cross-department problems get solved.

sessions.title: INTERLOCK Round 2
sessions.description: STATION-only interlocks. Same format — smaller groups, defined outcomes required. Focus on STATION-specific operational challenges.

sessions.title: Startup Cup Game 4
sessions.description: Quick team game.

sessions.title: DEPARTMENT WORKING SESSIONS
sessions.description: Each department gets 90 min of protected time with their immediate team. Focus: automation and AI workflows specific to their work. What can we automate right now? What AI tools should we adopt? What are the things we never have time for day-to-day? This is not a discussion session — teams should leave with something built, automated, or shipped. The most valuable block of the week per past DREAMCON feedback.
sessions.logistics: Dept managers prep agenda in advance. Each team should have a deliverable.

sessions.title: FOUNDER STORY: Elysium Robotics
sessions.description: Portfolio company founder shares their journey building in robotics. 15 min presentation + 5 min Q&A.
sessions.logistics: Rodrigo available after 11 AM only. Confirm deck.

sessions.title: Closing Remarks
sessions.description: Bryan wraps Day 3 — recap of AI day, preview of Day 4 celebrations.

sessions.title: FOUNDER STORY: AlphaRose
sessions.description: Portfolio company founder shares their journey building AlphaRose. 15 min presentation + 5 min Q&A.
sessions.logistics: Casey confirmed for Thursday 9 AM. 10-15 min + Q&A.

sessions.title: Startup Cup Game 5
sessions.description: Final regular-season game before the afternoon finals.

sessions.title: HECs + Fix It
sessions.description: 90-min block in two parts. First half (~60 min): each department documents their Hopes, Expectations, and Concerns for H2 2026. Managers facilitate. Results collected and shared with exec team post-DREAMCON. Second half (~30 min): each team picks their #1 most broken workflow and works to actually solve it in the room. Real solutions with owners — not just discussion. Framework: what's broken, what's the fix, who owns it, by when.
sessions.logistics: HEC templates distributed to managers in advance. Shared folder for submissions.

sessions.title: HECs + Fix It
sessions.description: 90-min block in two parts. First half (~60 min): each department documents their Hopes, Expectations, and Concerns for H2 2026. Managers facilitate. Results collected and shared with exec team post-DREAMCON. Second half (~30 min): each team picks their #1 most broken workflow and works to actually solve it in the room.
sessions.logistics: HEC templates distributed to managers in advance. Shared folder for submissions.

sessions.label: Staggered Exec AMA — Josh attends both

sessions.title: CF EXEC AMA
sessions.description: CF staff ask anonymous pre-submitted questions to the exec panel. No question is a dumb question. Candid, honest, transparent answers. COS moderates — reads questions by theme, panel responds. This is the most-requested session at every DREAMCON.
sessions.logistics: AMA submission form sent 3 weeks before. Questions organized by theme. COS has moderator notes.

sessions.title: Free Time — Emails & Recharge
sessions.description: STATION staff have free time while CF does their Exec AMA. Catch up on email, recharge, or continue department conversations from HECs.

sessions.title: Break — Josh walks from Captain America to Voltron
STATION EXEC AMA
sessions.description: Same format as CF AMA but for STATION staff. Anonymous questions, candid answers. Staggered so Josh can attend both. Addresses STATION-specific concerns about identity, direction, and morale.

sessions.title: CROSS-ENTITY INTERLOCK
sessions.description: Final joint working session of DREAMCON. CF + STATION together in small mixed groups with defined outcomes. Last chance to solve cross-entity challenges, align on shared initiatives, and set commitments before the half ends. Every group must leave with: problem statement, decision made, owner, deadline.

sessions.title: Startup Cup Finals + Winner Announcement
sessions.description: Final Startup Cup scores tallied across all 4 days of games + the MVP build. Winning team announced. Prizes awarded. The crowd goes wild.
sessions.logistics: Scoring spreadsheet finalized. Prizes ready.

sessions.title: CORE VALUE AWARDS + DCS26 Video
sessions.description: 6 Core Value Award winners announced — one per value. Each receives a $100 Amazon gift card. Staff nominated peers via form sent 2 weeks before DREAMCON. Past winners not eligible. DCS26 highlight video plays — captures the best moments of the week.
sessions.logistics: Gift cards purchased. Past winner list checked. Video edited and ready.

sessions.title: Letter to December
sessions.description: Each person writes a letter to themselves listing 3 things they need to accomplish over the next 6 months. Letters are sealed and collected. Opened together at DCW26. A personal accountability moment — what will you point to in December as your biggest impact?
sessions.logistics: Provide paper/envelopes. Collect and store sealed letters.

sessions.title: Alumni Spotlight
sessions.description: Final alumni speaker shares their post-CF career journey. Ends the spotlight series on a high note before Josh closes.

sessions.title: Closing Remarks — DREAMCON S26
sessions.description: Josh closes DREAMCON S26 — themes of the week, thank yous, shoutouts, looking ahead to H2 2026. Send everyone off with energy and clarity. The last thing people hear should make them proud to be on this team.

sessions.title: Team Dinners / Team Activities
sessions.description: Managers take their teams out for dinner. Protected team bonding time outside the office. No agenda — just enjoy each other's company.
sessions.logistics: Managers plan their own dinners.
